title: Massachusetts law about landlord and tenant | Mass.gov
content:
Massachusetts law about landlord and tenant

Laws, regulations, cases, and web sources on landlord and tenant law in general.

## Best bet

Legal tactics: Tenants' rights in Massachusetts, 9th ed., Massachusetts Law Reform Institute, 2025, with current updates (includes forms).
Housing, apartments, shelter, Mass. Legal Help. Various topical collections that contain information and resources relating to landlord-tenant issues, including: Eviction, Rent and security deposits, Repairs and bad conditions, Taking your landlord to court, Utilities, and more.

## Massachusetts laws

### Main laws
MGL c. 186 Estates for years and at will (Landlord-tenant law)

### Related laws
MGL c. 40P The Massachusetts rent control prohibition act
MGL c. 93, § 114 Psychologically impacted properties - Sellers and brokers don't have to tell buyers or tenants about a murder or suicide in the house, or about ghosts or other paranormal activity. However, they can't lie if they are asked about it.
MGL c. 112, § 87DDD 1/2 Real estate brokers and salesmen; persons finding dwelling accommodations for fee - Effective August 1, 2025 broker's fees must be paid by the party that hires the broker, which is usually the landlord.
MGL c. 140, § 22 Lodging house - A lodging house, or boarding house, is a house that rents out individual rooms to 4 or more people.
MGL c. 175, § 99 (Cl. 15th A) Standard form and content of policies or contracts insuring against loss or damage by fire - An insurance policy for a multi-unit apartment building must provide coverage up to a limit of $750 for each rental unit to cover the actual costs of relocation of any tenant or lawful occupant displaced by fire or by damage resulting from fire.

## Massachusetts regulations

### Department of Public Health
105 CMR 410 State sanitary code chapter II: Minimum standards of fitness for human habitation - Details the key requirements for housing, including kitchens, bathrooms, electricity, ceiling height, and much more.
105 CMR 410.260 Means of egress - Explains the responsibilities of both landlord and tenant for the maintenance and clearing of a residence's means of egress (such as stairs, pathways, fire escapes, etc.).
105 CMR 410.420 Habitability requirements - Is your apartment big enough? Includes the requirements for the amount of space per person.
105 CMR 410.550 Elimination of pests - Explains the responsibilities of both landlord and tenant for the control and elimination of pests.

### Attorney General
940 CMR 3 General regulations
940 CMR 3.17 Landlord-tenant - Regulations for consumer protection from unfair or deceptive acts or practices by landlords. Specifies prohibited acts by landlords in the areas of conditions, notices, rental agreements, security deposits, evictions, and more. Subsection (6)(e) outlines when a landlord may enter the tenant's home.

## Selected cases
Selected court cases on the topic of landlord-tenant issues.

## Forms
Landlord-tenant forms available online. Many print legal materials in the law libraries' collections also include forms.

## Report a code violation
In Massachusetts city and town local health authorities (LHA) enforce the state housing code, 105 CMR 410. Complaints regarding housing violations must be addressed to the LHA, which may be the board of health, health department, or inspectional services department. The LHA is responsible for conducting inspections, issuing orders to correct, and enforcement to ensure violations are corrected.

## Web sources

### General
Attorney General's guide to landlord and tenant rights, Mass. Attorney General. Covers terms of a rental agreement, security deposits and other payments, sanitary code requirements, evictions, and discrimination.

### Bed bugs and other pests
Bed bug guideline: Prevention and extermination, City of Boston, 2022. Explains responsibilities of landlords and tenants in dealing with bed bugs.
Pest control guidance and bed bug FAQs, Mass. Bureau of Climate and Environmental Health.

### Broker's fees
Frequently asked questions about residential rental broker's fees, Executive Office of Housing and Livable Communities. As of August 1, 2025, broker's fees for rental units must be paid by the person who hired the broker or salesperson.

### Mold
Guidance to control excess moisture and mold, Mass. Bureau of Climate and Environmental Health.

### Landlords
How to be a landlord in Massachusetts and avoid legal trouble, A. Joseph Ross, J.D., revised May 2020. This extensive document covers everything from screening tenants through eviction, including utilities, lead paint, security deposits, domestic violence, and more.

### Tenants
Guide me: I have a housing problem, Mass. Legal Resource Finder. Answer a few questions to understand your legal rights and responsibilities.
Housing code checklist (from Booklets: Representing yourself in an eviction), Mass. Law Reform Institute, 2025. The state Sanitary Code sets the minimum requirements for rental housing.
How to be a tenant in Massachusetts and avoid getting ripped off, A. Joseph Ross, J.D., revised May 2020. Covers all the key issues for a tenant, including security deposits, utilities, lead paint, landlords entering the apartment, and more.
Moving tips for tenants, A. Joseph Ross, J.D., October 2020. Precautions to take when moving, regardless of your relationship with your landlord.
